info.name: FixItNow API - Runnable Smoke Test
info.description: A single ordered, auto-chaining flow: run folders 01-11 top to bottom (or hit Run on the whole collection) and every request should pass. Folder 07 needs real Stripe/SSLCommerz keys configured on the server to go green; folder 10 only passes once per fresh `npm run seed`; folder 12 is reference-only and excluded from the runnable flow.

request.description: Uses a timestamped email so this is safe to run repeatedly without a 409 conflict.
Uses a timestamped email so this is safe to run repeatedly without a 409 conflict.

item.name: 07 - Payments (needs real Stripe/SSLCommerz keys on the server)
item.description: Actually completing a payment needs a real browser checkout, so it cannot be automated here - this folder only proves session creation works. The full paid -> in-progress -> completed -> reviewed lifecycle is demonstrated in folder 10 using pre-seeded data instead.

request.description: Only succeeds if the server has a real STRIPE_SECRET_KEY configured. With a placeholder key this correctly returns a 500 with a Stripe error message - that is expected, not a bug in this collection.

exec: pm.test("Status 201 (fails with 500 if STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is still a placeholder)", () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

item.name: 3. Get payment by id (only if step 1 succeeded)

request.description: Skipped/expected to fail if payment creation above did not succeed (no real provider keys configured yet).

item.name: 1. Create a second booking to cancel
10 - Paid booking lifecycle demo (uses seed data - run once per fresh seed)

request.description: If this returns an empty list, the seed data has already been consumed by a previous run of this folder. Run `npm run seed` again to reset it, then re-run this folder.

exec: pm.test("Found a PAID seed booking (re-run `npm run seed` if this fails)", () => pm.expect(json.data.length).to.be.above(0));

item.name: 3. Confirm its payment (already completed, so this is idempotent)

request.description: This booking was seeded as already paid, so confirm short-circuits and returns 200 without calling Stripe/SSLCommerz.

exec: pm.test("Status 201 (409 if this folder already ran once against this seed)", () => pm.response.to.have.status(201));

item.name: 12 - Reference only (do NOT include in Runner)
item.description: These are called by the payment gateways themselves, not by a client - deselect this folder before using Collection Runner, since they will not return success codes without a real gateway-signed request.

item.name: Stripe webhook (Stripe calls this, not you)
SSLCommerz success callback (gateway calls this, not you)
SSLCommerz fail callback (gateway calls this, not you)
SSLCommerz cancel callback (gateway calls this, not you)
SSLCommerz IPN (gateway calls this, not you)
Stripe checkout return (needs a real session_id from an actual checkout)
